title: Ayush, Unnati, Lakshya Bow Out of Japan Open
indian_angle: Top Indian shuttlers Ayush Shetty, Unnati Hooda, and Lakshya Sen faced early exits at the Japan Open, marking a disappointing day for India.
summary: Ayush Shetty, a recent Asia Championships finalist, fought hard but ultimately lost to second seed Kunlavut Vitidsarn in a three-game thriller, while Unnati Hooda and Lakshya Sen also exited.

title: Senior Badminton Ranking Tournament Returns to Tamil Nadu After Five Years
indian_angle: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament is set to be held in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, marking its return to the state after five years.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced the return of the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament to Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026, after a five-year hiatus.

title: BAI Releases Player Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued guidelines for player eligibility for the upcoming BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt, crucial for aspiring Indian junior shuttlers.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released the official player eligibility criteria for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, scheduled to take place in Cairo, Egypt, providing clarity for junior players and coaches.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: As India prepares to host the BWF World Championships in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger generations to ensure the sport's long-term popularity in the country.
summary: A feature article discusses the importance of engaging Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences for the success and future growth of badminton in India, especially with the upcoming BWF World Championships in Delhi.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented for Domestic Junior Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, aiming to enhance game dynamics.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships across U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, starting from June 30, 2026.
